headlines.title: Zohran Mamdani’s clout, a double Trump endorsement and more on the line in Tuesday’s primaries
headlines.description: The parties will also set up a handful of key House battleground races as voters in Maryland, New York, South Carolina and Utah pick nominees.

headlines.title: ACLU to monitor election certification as part of $50 million midterm effort
headlines.description: Half of the group’s spending will go toward ensuring the proper administration of elections in key states amid Trump’s efforts to exert greater influence over the process.

headlines.title: Photos show Trump’s name removed from Kennedy Center facade
headlines.description: A court ordered President Donald Trump’s name removed from the center by June 12. A tarp has since hidden the removal from the public.

headlines.title: Trump Defends Iran Concessions as Vance Touts Negotiations
headlines.description: Vice President JD Vance says his marathon first round of negotiations with Iran have set the foundation for a lasting peace deal. It comes as President Donald Trump faces criticism for Iran’s 60-day waiver for U.S. sanctions, allowing them to sell oil freely on the open market. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump claims proof of Reflecting Pool vandalism will be seen in court
headlines.description: President Trump is claiming without evidence that vandals are responsible for recent problems facing the newly renovated Reflecting Pool on the National Mall. NBC News’ Gary Grumbach reports on the algae growth and peeling paint that are plaguing the landmark.

headlines.title: Influential Former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan Dies at 100
headlines.description: Alan Greenspan, the influential economist who chaired the Federal Reserve for two decades and was the beloved husband of NBC’s Andrea Mitchell, has died at 100 after complications from Parkinson's disease. NBC’s Kristen Welker shares a look back at Greenspan’s life and his impact on the economy.

headlines.title: U.S. deportation tracker: Counting arrests, detentions and border crossings
headlines.description: President Donald Trump came into office promising the largest mass deportation in U.S history, targeting the more than 10 million unauthorized migrants living in the United States.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Top intelligence agency begins mass firings under new Trump appointee, source says
headlines.description: The terminations are taking place under acting Director Bill Pulte, whom President Donald Trump tasked with downsizing the National Intelligence Director’s Office.

headlines.title: U.S.-Iran talks set ‘good foundation’ for deal to end war, Vance says
headlines.description: Tehran agreed to allow U.N. nuclear inspectors into the country, the vice president said after a marathon round of negotiations in Switzerland.

headlines.title: Senate passes sweeping housing affordability bill with bipartisan support
headlines.description: The bipartisan legislation was crafted in both chambers and must now pass the House. It seeks to build more homes and prevent large investors from outbidding families.
